remaining_risks: list_projects: Any base path without '..' components (including absolute paths like /etc) is accepted; this can disclose directory layout anywhere the server process can read. Mitigation is operational trust, not an allowlist in code.

URL SSRF defense lives in testforge.input.url.parse_url (private IP checks, follow_redirects=False), not in analysis.py; http(s) strings bypass path validation but still fail closed on fetch for 127.0.0.1/private targets.
_is_private_url uses IPv4-only getaddrinfo(AF_INET); unusual IPv6 or resolution edge cases should be reviewed if IPv6 URLs are expected.
DNS rebinding between pre-check resolution and fetch is a known class of bypass for hostname-based SSRF guards; follow_redirects=False reduces redirect-based bypass only.
safeHref allows relative URLs starting with /, ., or #; same-origin implications depend on deployment; javascript: and data: are rejected by the allowlist.
